posts.title: GR11
posts.content: I am planning to walk the GR11 trail in Spain.  I will post updates to this website along the way.

posts.title: Days 1 - 3
posts.content:
I have finally started the GR11. After a quick stop in New York, I made my way to Irún, on the western coast of Spain, and started my walk 3 days ago. I'm now in a town called Elizondo, which is 60 km in.

I'm enjoying the trail so far. The heat has not been an issue. The heat dome that was sitting over Western Europe has eased, and the temperatures are less extreme than what I experienced in the desert of the PCT. I hope it stays that way! There are plenty of water sources, so I never have to carry more than about a liter at a time.

posts.title: Days 3 - 10
posts.content:
I am now about 210 km into my trip, and have stopped at a ski resort called Candanchú. It is mostly closed at this time of year, but there are a few places open. There are many large hotels, so I imagine it would be very busy during the winter.

In the last few days the landscape has transitioned from rolling hills to more rugged alpine terrain. The scenery is constantly improving, and the trail is also becoming more difficult. It reached an altitude of 2265 m today.

The weather has also changed significantly. There have been frequent thunder storms, which pass very quickly. The temperature has dropped off at the higher altitudes.

posts.title: Days 10 - 16
posts.content:
I'm now in a town called Parzán, and I am about 330 km in. The trail is becoming more difficult, with a lot of elevation change. Most ascents are followed by a steep descent, which makes progress slow. The average daily elevation gain over the last week has been about 1,350 m.

I have gone over some of the highest passes on the trail. Last week, I went over a 2,782 m pass. It was quite steep, and there were small patches of snow. Being so close to 3,000 m, I was keen to climb one of the nearby peaks. I took a detour up a peak called Infierno Occidental, which appeared straight forward. Unfortunately, the view from the saddle was deceptive. Near the top, it became a scramble which was beyond my ability, so I had to turn around. There are other high passes coming up, and I am hoping to find a 3000 m peak that is more manageable.

The trail has just taken me through a national park. This has been the busiest section of the trail. It's easy to see why. There are impressive valleys with steep cliff faces, which reminded me of Yosemite.
